parsed.summary: The commit adds an interactive menu to /glm:setup when already configured and renames the npm scope. The setup.md command change introduces user-facing interactive behavior without guardrails for non-interactive/silent failure modes, and the release_card.md changes are substantial but unvalidated documentation. The setup command silently proceeds on user dismissal which could leave users in a confused state.

findings.title: Interactive menu in setup command lacks non-interactive fallback handling
findings.body: The updated commands/setup.md adds an interactive choice menu when the project is already configured. If the hosting environment does not support interactive prompts (e.g., CI/CD, headless usage, or certain Claude client configurations), the user receives no clear error or fallback. This creates a silent failure path where setup appears to hang or no-op without feedback. The conditional branches based on user choice also mean a wrong selection could overwrite existing valid configuration with no confirmation or backup.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit non-interactive path or early-exit message when interactivity is unavailable. Consider prompting the user with a warning before overwriting existing configuration and document the expected behavior for non-interactive environments.

findings.title: npm scope rename may break existing installations referencing old scope
findings.body: The package.json changes the npm scope name. Any existing users who have the package installed under the old scope, or any documentation/scripts/references that depend on the old scope name, will break. There is no migration path or compatibility shim visible in the diff.
findings.recommendation: Verify that all references to the old scope name have been updated across the repository. If this is a published package, consider publishing a deprecation notice under the old name or documenting a migration step in CHANGELOG.md for existing users.

next_steps: Verify the setup command behavior in a non-interactive Claude environment to confirm it degrades gracefully rather than silently no-oping.
Confirm all references to the old npm scope name have been updated in any external documentation, CI configs, or dependent repositories.
Test the 'reconfigure' path end-to-end to ensure existing valid configuration is not silently lost on a menu mis-selection.
